mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 60% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand growth that supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with an accuracy above 55% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in the industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity constraints and supply bottlenecks that compress profit margins and cause negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns do not materialize when INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases, or the mechanism shows positive predictive power instead.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated CPIAUCSL combined with a low FEDFUNDS rate creates negative real yields that compress equity valuations, generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in 65% of triggered cases or the joint signal shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening economic activity and higher corporate revenue expectations, which systematically produce positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 4-year rolling window or 60-day forward returns are negative in 58% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing GDP growth and declining retail sales signals weakening aggregate demand that triggers immediate profit downgrades and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns fail to materialize when GDPC1 and RSAFS both decline, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expansion in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects resolving supply constraints and margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns do not occur in 60% of post-shock recovery periods when INDPRO rises, or the signal reverses direction.
